summary:
The primary determinant for discharge should involve consistent adequate urine production (>200ml/day) as it reflects stabilization of vital organ perfusion necessary before release. Other signs such as normalized blood pressure and absence of bleeding episodes also support but are secondary compared to this crucial marker reflecting systemic health status fully restored. \boxed{B}

The summary captures that while other factors contribute, maintaining sufficient urination shows organs can handle fluids safely now; essential for leaving without risking relapse into severe dengue states needing rehospitalization soon afterward due to poor long-term outcomes if prematurely released otherwise when kidneys etc aren't yet robust enough again despite seeming "better".

Answer choice: B
